growth_records.strain: Thermophilic AOB enrichment culture 'Butane50' (Guaymas Basin hydrothermal-vent sediment origin), sediment-free
growth_records.medium: Anoxic Tris-HCl-buffered artificial seawater (ASW) mineral medium; butane as sole carbon/energy source, sulfate as electron acceptor

up.units: mmol/L consumed (>98% of initially added 6 mmol/L consumed by ~120 d)
up.method: GC quantification of residual butane over incubation, experimental
calculated: 6 mmol/L / 120 d = 0.05 mmol/L/d; NOT biomass-normalized (no gDW reported)

up.units: mmol/L supplied/reduced as terminal electron acceptor (also assayed at 5 mmol/L; excess-butane assays reduced sulfate until ~20 mmol/L sulfide accumulated)

sec.units: mmol/L accumulated (sulfate reduction stopped once ~20 mmol/L sulfide accumulated in excess-butane assays)
accumulated over incubation (endpoint concentration not stated numerically in main text; tracked via delta13C)
sec.method: product of complete butane oxidation, experimental (qualitative)

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: CO2->butane back flux relative to net AOB (f-/fnet)
mfa.units: per mil (permil) of net AOB rate (highest proportion)
mfa.note: 13C back-flux measured via delta13C enrichment of residual butane pool from 13C-DIC label; demonstrates reversibility of the whole AOB pathway; ~1 order of magnitude lower than AOM back flux

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: reducing equivalents: butane oxidation vs sulfate reduction ratio
mfa.units: dimensionless (mol e- released / mol e- consumed)
mfa.note: average ratio 1.04, consistent with net AOB stoichiometry coupling butane oxidation to sulfate->sulfide reduction; reducing equivalents per butane = 2 XH2 + 5 NADH + 4 F420H2 (Table 1 / Supplementary)

product_yields.note: no biomass yield determined; electron balance (butane oxidation : sulfate reduction) ratio = 1.04 reported instead

growth_records.cite: Song-Can Chen et al. Back flux during anaerobic oxidation of butane supports archaea-mediated alkanogenesis. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-53932-9. [PMC11543930]
growth_records.method: not reported (no biomass-normalized growth rate or doubling time given; substrate consumption followed volumetrically over ~120 d batch incubation)
growth_records.snippet: Experiments were done with a thermophilic AOB enrichment culture, culture Butane50... labeling of the residual butane pool peaked after approximately 120 days of incubation, when over 98% of the initially added butane was consumed... the hi
growth_records.notes: Archaeal (prokaryote) enrichment co-culture; anaerobic butane oxidation coupled to sulfate reduction at 50 C (stated optimum growth temperature of Ca. S. butanivorans). Measured (experimental) quantities: butane supplied 6 mmol/L with >98% consumed by ~120 d; sulfate 28 mmol/L (also 5 mmol/L assays); excess-butane (30 mmol/L) assays reduced sulfate until ~20 mmol/L sulfide accumulated. DERIVED volumetric butane rate = 6 mmol/L / 120 d = 0.05 mmol/L/d (arithmetic shown; NOT gDW-normalized because no biomass/gDW reported, so cannot compute qS in mmol/gDW/h). No growth rate, doubling time, dilution rate, pH, or full medium recipe given in main text (ASW medium composition cited from refs 41,42). Back-flux MFA ratio f-/fnet = 1.5-2.5 permil of net AOB rate; electron-equivalent ratio butane-oxidation:sulfate-reduction = 1.04. Full ASW composition not in main text -> medium_composition_stated:false. Other three assigned papers had NO experimental prokaryote rates: PMC11525543 (sponge holobiont) reports only FBA-PREDICTED fluxes for uncultured symbionts N. spongiisocia (archaeon) and Z. abyssi (bacterium), no measured rates; PMC11536133 is an atmospheric-CH4 box-model/isotope study, no cultured prokaryote; PMC11532494 is a bioinformatic survey of bacterial classical deacylases (enzymology), no growth physiology.
